video:video.video:title: Samuel's 'SAMUELiTO' blends K-pop with Latin roots
video:video.video:description: Korean-Mexican-American singer Samuel explains how "ZIGI-ZIGI-ZIGI," a track from his latest release "SAMUELiTO," exploded spontaneously in the studio. (June 9)

video:video.video:title: 'House of the Dragon' star Emma D'Arcy: It's time for a matriarchy
video:video.video:description: "House of the Dragon" star Emma D'Arcy says "Give a matriarchy a go."

video:video.video:title: Xi makes rare visit to North korea as he and Kim seek stronger China-North Korea ties
video:video.video:description: China's President Xi Jinping and North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un underscored their commitment to deepen cooperation and rebuild their complicated traditional alliance.

video:video.video:title: Former White House chief of staff's bike tour in New Hampshire tests a 2028 presidential bid
video:video.video:description: For Rahm Emanuel, the road to the White House runs through the uphill climbs of rural New Hampshire.

video:video.video:title: No respite from intense heatwave for outdoor laborers in Lahore
video:video.video:description: Lahore is experiencing an intense heatwave with temperatures expected to reach up to 47 degrees Celsius (117 degrees Fahrenheit) this week.

video:video.video:title: 'Disclosure Day' star Emily Blunt believes in aliens
video:video.video:description: Actor Emily Blunt discusses the probability of extra-terrestrial life, as she joins director Steven Spielberg, Josh O'Connor, Colman Domingo and Eve Hewson at the New York premiere of "Disclosure Day."

video:video.video:title: Engineers build AI-powered ultrasound wristband that enables realtime control of robotic hands, VR
video:video.video:description: Scientists have developed an ultrasound wristband that uses artificial intelligence to translate images of the wrist into real-time control of a dexterous robotic hand – or manipulate objects in a virtual environment.

video:video.video:title: Rahm Emanuel's New Hampshire bike tour tests 2028 presidential bid
video:video.video:description: Rahm Emanuel has been making moves in New Hampshire, hinting at a possible return to Washington politics, by biking more than 100 miles across the state, which typically holds the first presidential primary.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 9
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 9th: Trump says pilots rescued after helicopter crash in Strait of Hormuz; Trump booed at NBA Finals game; Crews search for earthquake survivors in the Philippines; Four states hold primaries on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Xi makes rare visit to Pyongyang as he and Kim seek stronger China-North Korea ties
video:video.video:description: China's President Xi Jinping and North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un underscored their commitment to deepen cooperation and rebuild their complicated traditional alliance.

video:video.video:title: 20,000 people displaced by Philippines earthquake that killed at least 37
video:video.video:description: Rescuers searched ruined buildings in the southern Philippines on Tuesday to ensure no one was still trapped a day after one of the strongest earthquakes to hit the country in a half-century killed at least 37 people and displaced over 20,000.

video:video.video:title: 20,000 people displaced by Philippines earthquake that killed at least 37
video:video.video:description: Rescuers searched ruined buildings in the southern Philippines Tuesday to ensure no one was still trapped a day after one of the strongest earthquakes to hit the country in a half-century killed at least 37 people and displaced over 20,000.

video:video.video:title: Joint investigation into ICE detainees describing medical neglect
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of detainees across at least 33 states allege in federal lawsuits that immigration detention facilities are failing to provide adequate medical care, an investigation by KFF Health News and The Associated Press exposes.

video:video.video:title: Japanese city captures bear that caused fear and school closures
video:video.video:description: A wandering bear that scared residents of a Japanese city just north of Tokyo was captured on Tuesday after several days of panic during which all schools were closed.

video:video.video:title: Toronto neighborhood challenges World Cup visitors to join The Great Beaver Quest
video:video.video:description: Thousands are flocking to a Toronto park to catch a glimpse of a beaver sculpture named Doug that is part of a new World Cup-themed contest. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Maine voters share their thoughts on US Senate candidates Graham Platner and Susan Collins
video:video.video:description: Maine voters have shared their thoughts on U.S. Senate candidates of their choice during the primary election on Tuesday. (AP Video shot by Rodrique Ngowi)

video:video.video:title: Thousands flee Lebanese city under renewed escalation and threat of Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: Cars packed with mattresses, luggage and household belongings stretched for miles along Lebanon's coastal highway as residents fled the southern city of Tyre following a latest Israeli warning.

video:video.video:title: Air Canada pilot charged after allegedly flying without proper license between 2009 and 2025
video:video.video:description: Geoffrey Wall, of Barrie, Ontario, is alleged to have operated as an airline captain between 2009 and 2025 without a license to fly large commercial passenger planes, according to Peel Regional Police.

video:video.video:title: NASA unveils Artemis III astronauts to test technology for a future moon landing
video:video.video:description: NASA revealed the crew for its Artemis III mission - NASA’s Randy Bresnik, Frank Rubio, Andre Douglas and the European Space Agency’s Luca Parmitano - the next step in the space agency’s plan to eventually land astronauts on the moon.

video:video.video:title: More swings for AI stocks drag Wall Street back on the roller coaster
video:video.video:description: Drops for AI stocks drowned out the benefit of lower oil prices, and most stocks in the S&P 500 rose. Treasury yields edged lower in the bond market.

video:video.video:title: Federal judge strikes down Trump’s $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas
video:video.video:description: A federal judge struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 H-1B visa fee, reversing a previous court decision that upheld the hike designed to protect American jobs.

video:video.video:title: Israelis and Iranians weigh what comes next as countries appear to pause strikes
video:video.video:description: Israelis and Iranians showed conflicted emotions on Tuesday as the two countries appeared to end the latest exchange of strikes; some expressed optimism, while others remained skeptical about what comes next.

video:video.video:title: Christian Watson discusses personal goals for the 2026 season
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers wide receiver Christian Watson discusses personal goals for the 2026 season now that he has signed a four-year contract extension.

video:video.video:title: Survivor saw floors 'going up and down' as powerful earthquake hit in southern Philippines
video:video.video:description: Residents in General Santos assessed the damage to their homes and buildings on Tuesday a day after a powerful earthquake struck the southern Philippines.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 9
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, June 9: Trump says US must respond to downed helicopter; Pope Leo greets thousands in Barcelona; Protests in Kenya over US proposed Ebola center; Carney says Canadian-built bridge across Detroit River that Trump threatened will open.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US 'must' respond after Iran shot down US Army helicopter near Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump blamed Iran for shooting down a U.S. Army helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday and said the United States must respond to the attack.

video:video.video:title: Stadium workers near LA say they have a tentative deal, averting strike ahead of World Cup
video:video.video:description: Workers represented by UNITE HERE Local 11 said they were seeking pay increases, protections from subcontracting and security on the job amid ramped-up immigration enforcement under President Donald Trump’s administration.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia Stadium ready for World Cup heat with cooling benches installed
video:video.video:description: Philadelphia’s World Cup stadium is ready for the summer heat, with cooling benches installed on the sidelines to help players cope with high temperatures expected during the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Albania's prime minister vows to press ahead with Trump family-linked coastal resort
video:video.video:description: Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama is vowing to press ahead with a massive coastal development linked to Jared Kushner, U.S.

video:video.video:title: North Korean state TV shows leader Kim Jong Un welcoming Chinese President Xi in Pyongyang
video:video.video:description: North Korean state television on Tuesday broadcast footage of Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit to Pyongyang, which started on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Albanians hold 10th of protest in Tirana against tourism project linked to Trump's son-in-law
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Albanians on Tuesday marched through the capital Tirana against a tourism development project linked to US President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner.

video:video.video:title: Graham Platner wins the Democratic primary for US Senate in Maine, will face GOP Sen. Susan Collins
video:video.video:description: Graham Platner won the Maine Democratic primary for U.S. Senate on Tuesday, setting up a high-stakes race against Republican Sen. Susan Collins.

video:video.video:title: Police are investigating a large burning cross at a Chicago park
video:video.video:description: A large, burning cross was discovered at a Chicago park on Tuesday afternoon, and police said they are investigating how it ended up there and the motive behind it.

video:video.video:title: Supporters of Karmelo Anthony demonstrate as teen convicted of murder for stabbing another athlete
video:video.video:description: A jury rejected Anthony’s claims of self-defense during a confrontation with Austin Metcalf in stadium bleachers last year.

video:video.video:title: A look at the hidden treasures of Barcelona's Sagrada Familia, ahead of Pope Leo's visit
video:video.video:description: The unique grandeur of the Sagrada Familia — the stone somehow carved to look lush as flora, the sandcastle-like spires, the color-flooded interior — means that even those who work and worship at Barcelona’s world famous basilica each and every day can still be surprised with some fresh wonder.

video:video.video:title: Graham Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate nomination despite multiple controversies
video:video.video:description: Graham Platner has secured Maine's Democratic Senate nomination, setting up a high-stakes battle against longtime incumbent Republican Sen.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers continue search for survivors following 7.8-magnitude quake that hit the Philippines
video:video.video:description: Rescuers searched ruined buildings in the southern Philippines on Wednesday to ensure no one was still trapped two days after one of the strongest earthquakes to hit the country in a half-century killed at least 37 people and displaced more than 32,000.

video:video.video:title: Tremor felt in General Santos, Philippines, two days after deadly quake hit the country
video:video.video:description: Rescuers who were searching ruined buildings in the southern Philippines after one of the strongest earthquakes to hit the country, felt another smaller earthquake as they were working on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: US and Iran launch strikes after Trump blames Tehran for helicopter crash
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched airstrikes and Iran retaliated following the crash of an Army helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz that U.S.

video:video.video:title: ICC chief prosecutor suspended pending decision by oversight body on sexual misconduct allegations
video:video.video:description: In an unprecedented move, the embattled chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court was suspended from his duties late Monday, after the court’s oversight body referred British barrister Karim Khan for disciplinary proceedings.

video:video.video:title: Kenyan police move to clear latest protest outside site of proposed US Ebola quarantine facility
video:video.video:description: Kenyan police clashed with protesters on Tuesday near an air base which will host a proposed quarantine facility for U.S. Ebola patients.

video:video.video:title: Seattle's goal to open 500 shelter units for the homeless before the World Cup fell short
video:video.video:description: New Seattle Mayor Katie Wilson had pledged 500 new shelter units for the city's homeless before the World Cup started.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia mixes World Cup excitement with America 250th events
video:video.video:description: World Cup travelers to Philadelphia could find themselves stepping into two major celebrations at once.

video:video.video:title: No kisses, no hugs: How Bunia weddings are changing as Ebola spreads
video:video.video:description: Despite the Ebola outbreak in Bunia, in the Democratic Republic of Congo, some families were determined to go ahead with their relatives’ weddings.

video:video.video:title: Matty Matheson on saying goodbye to 'The Bear'
video:video.video:description: While attending the "Toy Story 5" premiere, Matty Matheson says he's thankful for the lifelong friends he's made on the set of "The Bear." (June 9)

video:video.video:title: In Barcelona, Pope Leo gets welcomed by a human tower
video:video.video:description: A group of more than 130 people in Barcelona gave a unique welcome to Pope Leo XIV on Tuesday night -- forming a human tower.

video:video.video:title: Bodies removed in Johannesburg after a dozen people killed by attackers in late-night mass shooting
video:video.video:description: South African police say that multiple attackers killed 12 people and wounded at least nine in a late-night mass shooting in the city of Johannesburg.

video:video.video:title: World Cup ref denied entry to US had 'earned right to officiate at highest level'
video:video.video:description: A World Cup referee from Somalia who was denied entry to the United States after arriving in Miami and subsequently cut from the tournament by FIFA was set to make history for his country.

video:video.video:title: World Cup excitement in Mexico tempered by security concerns
video:video.video:description: As Mexico prepares to host World Cup matches, excitement among fans and residents is being accompanied by questions about security and whether the country is ready for the influx of visitors.

video:video.video:title: Some African burial societies now focus on helping the living too
video:video.video:description: Burial societies in Zimbabwe and other parts of Africa are turning into lifelines for daily survival.

video:video.video:title: Tom Hanks, Tim Allen launch 'Toy Story 5'
video:video.video:description: Joan Cusack, Wallace Shawn and Tony Hale among cast gathering to launch the latest adventure in the much loved franchise, in Los Angeles. (June 10)

video:video.video:title: Ecuadorian artisans struggle to preserve traditional hand-woven strainers
video:video.video:description: Artisans in Guangopolo, Ecuador, are struggling to keep the traditional craft of weaving horsehair sieves alive. 50 years ago, about 500 families made a living from the craft, selling up to 600 sieves a month.

video:video.video:title: US military disable oil tanker violating blockade in the Gulf of Oman, CENTCOM says
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Wednesday that it disabled an oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman. The Palau-flagged vessel was suspected of trying to violate the American blockade, the latest action by the Trump administration to try to push Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits prison on outskirts of Barcelona with message of hope for inmates
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited a Barcelona prison Wednesday, keeping up a tradition of Pope Francis, who often used his foreign trips to minister to those who can’t join in the public celebrations when a pope comes to town.

video:video.video:title: Trump says 'I love the inflation,' claims US taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump called the latest inflation numbers “great," insisting prices will “come down like a rock” once the war with Iran ends.

video:video.video:title: Bill Gates to testify before panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files
video:video.video:description: Bill Gates has arrived on Capitol Hill to appear before a congressional panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files, becoming the latest powerful figure linked to the disgraced financier to testify.

video:video.video:title: What is an IPO? What to know as the potentially biggest IPO in history, SpaceX, hits the market
video:video.video:description: Anyone following SpaceX's plans to sell shares to the public is likely to hear terms thrown around that describe steps and components of an initial public offering.

video:video.video:title: UK and US diplomats stress Iran's influence in the Middle East extends to terror groups
video:video.video:description: The United Kingdom and the United States told members of the United Nations Security Council Wednesday that Iran's actions to halt traffic in the Strait of Hormuz must cease.

video:video.video:title: Street in The Hague drenched in orange as excitement builds in Netherlands for World Cup
video:video.video:description: As the clock ticks down to the Netherlands' opening World Cup match, the country is gripped once more by orange fever.

video:video.video:title: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran, as Tehran attacks countries in region
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump’s warnings at the White House and on social media came hours after Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan — all of which host U.S. troops — came under Iranian fire.

video:video.video:title: San Francisco not expecting big influx of tourists for World Cup games
video:video.video:description: The San Francisco Bay Area is preparing to host six World Cup games, but tourism officials don’t expect a big influx of international visitors due to geopolitical issues.

video:video.video:title: Dallas Stadium unveils World Cup transformation ahead of opening matches
video:video.video:description: Dallas Stadium will host the first North Texas World Cup match on June 14th between the Netherlands and Japan.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Impact of Brendan Sorsby's court win on college sports and gambling
video:video.video:description: Texas Tech transfer quarterback Brendan Sorsby has won a temporary injunction against the NCAA over his gambling suspension.

video:video.video:title: Israel tightens its grip on east Jerusalem with evictions and demolitions
video:video.video:description: Fakhri Abu Diab fought for decades to save his home. But when Israeli authorities arrived with bulldozers two years ago, he was powerless to stop them.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo gets welcomed by a human tower in Barcelona
video:video.video:description: Human towers, or “castells” in the Catalan language, are not only a feat of equilibrium, strength and teamwork, but a crucial part of the proud identity in this northeastern Spanish region.

video:video.video:title: A new generation of peregrine falcons fledge from their Sagrada Familia home
video:video.video:description: Two peregrine falcon fledglings have flown from their nests at the top of Barcelona’s famed Sagrada Familia, leaving behind crowds with binoculars, and live stream cameras which have been following their every move.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift and Randy Newman perform duet at 'Toy Story 5' LA premiere
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift performs her "Toy Story 5" song "I Knew It, I Knew You," before being joined by Randy Newman to duet franchise theme "You Got a Friend in Me." (June 10)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, June 10th: Iran launches attacks on Kuwait, Bahrain; Graham Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate nomination; Burning cross found in Chicago park; Bill Gates to testify to House Committee about Epstein files.

video:video.video:title: Ian McKellen honors the home of Laurence Olivier
video:video.video:description: One British acting great, Ian McKellen, unveils a blue plaque on the childhood home of another, Sir Laurence Olivier, at 22 Lupus Street in Pimlico, London. (10 June)

video:video.video:title: UFC heavyweight Derrick Lewis on fighting at the White House
video:video.video:description: UFC heavyweight Derrick Lewis on fighting at the White House

video:video.video:title: UFC heavyweight Derrick Lewis on fighting at the White House
video:video.video:description: UFC heavyweight Derrick Lewis on fighting at the White House

video:video.video:title: Trump says he'll back Sen. Collins in Maine, blasting Platner as a 'thug' and 'pig'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he’s backing Republican incumbent Susan Collins in the Maine Senate race despite their differences, bashing her opponent, Democrat Graham Platner, as a "thug" and an "outright pig."

video:video.video:title: UFC fighter Michael Chandler on UFC fight at the White House
video:video.video:description: UFC fighter Michael Chandler on UFC fight at the White House

video:video.video:title: UFC fighter Sean O’Malley on fight night
video:video.video:description: UFC fighter Sean O’Malley on fight night

video:video.video:title: Green Bay Packers' Tucker Kraft on coming back from injury
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers tight end Tucker Kraft believes he should return to peak form once he's back on the field after recovering from a torn anterior cruciate ligament.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, June 10th: Trump says Tehran will 'pay the price' for stalled talks; US Inflation hits 4.2% in May; Mass shooting kills 12 and injures 9 in a Johannesburg suburb; Scientists discover a deep whale graveyard.

video:video.video:title: What Knicks fever looks like in New York City
video:video.video:description: The Knicks have New York buzzing. We hit the streets to see what Knicks fever looks like across the city.

video:video.video:title: Keith Urban's 'bulletproof' new album
video:video.video:description: Keith Urban talks about his new album, "Flow State," which features 10 cover songs, guest appearances from John Mayer and Little Big Town, and one original track, "We Go Back," featuring Michael McDonald.

video:video.video:title: Somali World Cup referee denied entry to US arrives home to hero's welcome
video:video.video:description: A World Cup referee from Somalia who was denied entry to the United States has returned to his country's capital of Mogadishu, where he was received by a crowd of supporters and officials.

video:video.video:title: Emily Blunt says you can always bet on Spielberg for a good movie night
video:video.video:description: Josh O’Connor dishes on his favorite Steven Spielberg films, while Emily Blunt says the director's catalog is always a wonderful choice for a good movie night.

video:video.video:title: Bill Gates arrives to testify before panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files
video:video.video:description: Bill Gates has arrived on Capitol Hill to appear before a congressional panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files, becoming the latest powerful figure linked to the disgraced financier to testify.

video:video.video:title: Stars bring 'Toy Story 5' to Hollywood
video:video.video:description: Tom Hanks and Tim Allen launch the latest adventure in the much loved "Toy Story" franchise.

video:video.video:title: Somali World Cup referee denied entry to US arrives home to hero's welcome
video:video.video:description: A World Cup referee from Somalia who was denied entry to the United States was received by a crowd of supporters and officials Wednesday as he arrived in Somalia’s capital of Mogadishu.

video:video.video:title: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth arrives in Cuba in shorts and t-shirt
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth arrived at Leeward Point Field in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Police say 12 people dead and nine wounded in late-night shooting in South Africa
video:video.video:description: A group of armed attackers drove into a poor neighborhood in South Africa’s biggest city, got out of a minivan and opened fire at residents in a late-night attack in Johannesburg that killed 12 people and wounded at least nine, police said Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: UN chief warns escalating attacks and rhetoric risk 'full war' in Iran and Gulf region
video:video.video:description: António Guterres told the U.N. Security Council Wednesday that the ceasefire in the Gulf region “is more like a lesser fire,” as the world has witnessed in the last 48 hours, with the downing of a U.S. helicopter, and the U.S. and Iran now trading attacks.

video:video.video:title: Honda recalls over 880,000 cars due to rear suspension issue
video:video.video:description: Honda is recalling more than 800,000 vehicles because rear suspension components may fail and cause drivers to lose control, increasing the chances of a crash or injury.

video:video.video:title: John McEnroe optimistic about Knicks in NBA finals
video:video.video:description: Tennis star John McEnroe says he's "been waiting a long time" for the Knicks to be contenders for the NBA championship. (June 10)

video:video.video:title: Stabbing suspect appears in court after anti-immigrant protests in Northern Ireland
video:video.video:description: A man from Sudan has appeared in court after being charged with attempted murder in a stabbing attack in Belfast that left a man seriously injured.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV sits in cockpit for flight from Madrid to Barcelona
video:video.video:description: The pope appeared to be having a great time sitting in a jump seat between the two pilots, even talking to passengers on the intercom.

video:video.video:title: Pakistan army helicopter crashes in Kashmir due to technical fault, killing all on board
video:video.video:description: A Pakistani army MI-17 helicopter crashed due to a technical fault in Pakistan-controlled Kashmir on Wednesday, killing all military personnel on board, the military said.

video:video.video:title: Bill Gates testifies to House Oversight Committee about his ties to Epstein, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Bill Gates says he made an error in judgment by ever meeting with Jeffrey Epstein as the Microsoft co-founder faces questions behind closed doors from lawmakers about his relationship with the disgraced financier.

video:video.video:title: Amnesty accuses Israel's government of 'ethnic cleansing' of Palestinians from the West Bank
video:video.video:description: Amnesty International accused Israel on Wednesday of carrying out a campaign of “ethnic cleansing” of Palestinians from the occupied West Bank with the intention to annex the Palestinian territory.

video:video.video:title: Nora Fatehi’s key ingredients for a World Cup song: rhythm, celebration, and a winning feel
video:video.video:description: World Cup opening ceremony performer, Nora Fatehi reveals what fans can expect from her FIFA 2026 official song ‘Siir Siir.' (June 11)

video:video.video:title: Nora Fatehi releases FIFA World Cup 2026 official song ‘Siir Siir'
video:video.video:description: World Cup opening ceremony performer, Nora Fatehi talks about her FIFA 2026 official song ‘Siir Siir.' (June 11)

video:video.video:title: Police blast water cannons at Belfast protesters as unrest flares again after stabbing
video:video.video:description: Police blasted water cannons Wednesday at protesters in Northern Ireland who set small fires and hurled bricks, rocks and bottles at them during a second night of violence over a brutal stabbing on a Belfast street.

video:video.video:title: US military’s Central Command says it launched more strikes on Iran on second day of renewed fire
video:video.video:description: US military’s Central Command says it launched more strikes on Iran on second day of renewed fire

video:video.video:title: Faithful find joy and importance in Pope Leo's visit to mountainous Montserrat abbey
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV participated in the reading of the rosary at an ancient mountain-top abbey in Montserrat on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Mexico City subway station goes viral over chandeliers
video:video.video:description: Mexico City's subway is known for its crowds and fast pace. But lately, one station has become famous for something unexpected: chandeliers.

video:video.video:title: Postcard helps identify British soldier and track down his family a century after death in WWI
video:video.video:description: A postcard belonging to a World War I soldier whose body was found with five comrades during an excavation has helped reunite distant descendants more than a century after his death on the Western Front.

video:video.video:title: Sagrada Familia lights up as Pope Leo blesses the world's tallest church in Spain
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has celebrated the Sagrada Familia Basilica as a masterpiece of “stones, colors and light.”

video:video.video:title: Northern English town set for by-election which may give Andy Burnham a path to be next UK leader
video:video.video:description: Nearly 75,000 voters in Ashton-in-Makerfield will cast ballots in a contest that may well pick the UK’s next prime minister, or plunge Britain's febrile politics into even more turmoil.
